title: Pilosella proceriformis
uses: Pilosella proceriformis, a species of flowering plant in the dandelion family, is used in traditional folk medicine. The plant has been reported to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may contribute to its potential medicinal uses. It is used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and skin conditions. In some traditional remedies, Pilosella proceriformis is used to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory issues. However, its effectiveness and safety for human consumption have not been extensively scientifically studied.
taxonomy: Pilosella proceriformis is classified in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. It belongs to the genus Pilosella, which includes over 100 species of plants. The genus Pilosella is further divided into several species based on their characteristics, with Pilosella proceriformis being one of the notable species. Pilosella proceriformis is a member of the tribe Cichorieae, a group of plants that are related to the chicory and dandelion families. The exact classification of Pilosella proceriformis can be further refined at the species level, but it is generally considered to be a member of the Asteraceae family.

parts: Pilosella proceriformis, also known as the false sowthistle, is a plant used in traditional medicine. preparations: Pilosella proceriformis is not commonly reported to be used in herbal preparations. intro: Pilosella proceriformis, also known as Red Hawkweed, is a perennial plant belonging to the Asteraceae family, characterized by its rosettes of narrow, pointed leaves and tall, erect stems bearing red or pink flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing pain and inflammation, as well as protecting against oxidative stress and cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Pilosella proceriformis include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Pilosella proceriformis is commonly used to prepare teas, tinctures, and infusions, often in combination with other herbs, to support joint health and reduce inflammation.
